description: Full-AOI NEON benchmark targets (issue #202 legs 1+5). Unlike targets.json (ONE pinned densest shard/target), each target here dispatches ALL shards over the AOP_NEON.geojson box, so the recorded numbers are the true full-AOI cost, not a single-cell probe. Reset-series axes per issue #202: index_backend (inline vs sidecar) x aoi_mask (on vs off), o9 shards only, tdigest/sharded/granule_workers=4 fixed. Recorded PER RELEASE (not per merge). run_full_aoi_benchmark.py builds the shardmap over the AOI+temporal+cmr below from a catalog (--catalog), then dispatches every shard. In CI it builds from the PINNED catalog catalogs/cat_neon.parquet -- the AOI-bbox+temporal subset of the local full ATL03 catalog (2089 granules, the cat_88s precedent), so the release harness runs offline (no CMR) and the granule set is FIXED across releases: the per-release series then tracks CODE change over the whole AOI, not CMR data drift. Rebuild it by prefiltering the full catalog to this manifest's aoi bbox + temporal (run_full_aoi_benchmark._prefilter) and Catalog.to_geoparquet. The whole-AOI build fans to 4 o9 shards (densest 92 granules -- distinct from targets.json's single CMR-pinned densest cell at 66). NOT consumed by run_benchmark.py or the single-shard drift test; a NEW harness owns it (leg 4a).

dispatch.note: Default dispatch target; the workflow overrides region/function from the shared BENCHMARK_* repo vars (the same ones the per-merge run uses). No account is pinned -- the release run reuses the per-merge benchmark role, and run_benchmark.py asserts no account either. A fork may add an optional expect_account here to make the harness assert the caller account before billing (it no-ops when absent).

full_aoi_neon_o9_hive._comment: Hive-layout regression arm over ALL 4 NEON shards (issue #240 item 2, unblocked by issue #236). RECORD-ONLY like the rest of the release leg (espg ruling on PR #242: a flaky-CMR release must never be blocked). parity_with names the flat sibling target (same config modulo store_layout): after this target's dispatch the harness reads BOTH stores back and records per-shard array-content parity (parity_ok + JSON detail) -- the flat sibling must run first, so this target is listed last (targets run in manifest order). Excluded from the 2x2 cost panels via the store_layout column; its rows live in the release series parquet.
